entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - しきい値の設定
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、しきい値を設定し、その値を超えているのか、例えばある目標値を達成しているのかや予算比がプラスなのかマイナスなのか、といったことをぱっとみて把握できるようにする方法についてご紹介します。

 
 目次：

  • SAP Analytics Cloudにおけるしきい値の設定
  • テーブルでのしきい値による色分け方法［ハンズオン］

※ベースとなるサンプルストーリーの作成手順は、はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityでご紹介しています。

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2026年2月に作成しています。）

SAP Analytics Cloudにおけるしきい値の設定

SAP Analytics Cloudでは、しきい値を設けて範囲ごとに色分けして表現することができます。範囲は複数設定することができるため、細かくしきい値を分けて管理することができます。また色の表現方法は、数字の横にマークとして表現するだけでなく、数値字体や背景の色を反映させることができます。

詳細はヘルプページをご覧ください：テーブルでのしきい値の使用 | SAP Help Portal

テーブルでのしきい値による色分け方法［ハンズオン］

このハンズオンは、はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - 差異の表示 の「テーブルでの差異の追加方法［ハンズオン］」の続きになります。上記のハンズオンを完了してから以下のハンズオンを実施してください。

0. はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - 差異の表示で編集したストーリーを編集モードで開こう！

0.1. ナビゲーションバーのファイルより作成したストーリーを開き、「編集」をクリックします。

1. テーブル上の予算比にしきい値を設定して色分けしよう！

しきい値は、テーブルに直接追加するか、ビルダパネルを使用して追加することができますが、今回はテーブルに直接追加する方法をご紹介します。

1.1. テーブルの「予算比」のセルを右クリックし、「しきい値を追加」>「＋新規しきい値」をクリックします。

1.2. 今回は予算比がプラスの場合は緑色、マイナスの場合は赤色となるようにしきい値を設定します。
新規しきい値の範囲にて、最小に「0」と入力し、「範囲の追加」をクリックします。

1.3.  新しく追加した範囲の色を赤色に変更します。

1.4. 赤色の範囲の最大に「0」と入力し、Dateのフィルタを「×」で解除して「適用」をクリックします。

1.5. 予算比がプラスかマイナスかがぱっとみてわかるようになりました！

1.6. (オプション) 右パネル（スタイルパネル）を開き、「しきい値のスタイル」をお好きなものに変更します。

 

 

今回はしきい値のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

entry.title: Dynamic RAP Actions Explained: Abstract Entities Powering Flexible Action Buttons
entry.content:
Introduction--

In the RESTful ABAP Programming Model (RAP), actions play a crucial role in triggering custom business logic on business objects — for example, approving, rejecting, or performing special updates on records. 

Sometimes, these actions require user inputs, such as selecting a reason code, approval type, or custom parameter value before execution. To achieve this, RAP provides parameterized actions, and to enhance usability, we can use an abstract entity to define the parameter structure along with a dropdown (value help). 

In this blog, we’ll explore how to build a parameterized RAP action that displays a dropdown list for user selection, powered by an abstract entity. This approach keeps the core logic clean, reusable, and consistent with RAP’s “clean core” principles. 

Objective--

By the end of this blog, you’ll learn how to: -

  • Define an abstract entity to represent the action parameters 
  • Provide a dropdown for the parameter field 
  • Link the abstract entity to the RAP action definition 
  • Implement the behavior logic to handle user input 
  • Execute and test the action from the Fiori Elements UI

Business Scenario Overview--

You are designing a Purchase Order (PO) Management RAP application, consisting of a Header and Item structure. 

The goal: -

  • To release purchase orders (POs) from Fiori using a parameterized action. 
  • The action uses an abstract entity to capture runtime parameters (like release flag). 

Database Tables--

Header Table: ZBRT_T_PO_HRD 

This table stores high-level details of a purchase order. 

@EndUserText.label : 'Purchase order header table' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zbrt_t_po_hrd { 

key mandt    : abap.clnt not null;
key ebeln    : ebeln not null;
bukrs        : abap.char(4);
supplier     : abap.char(10);
bsart        : abap.char(4);
lifnr        : abap.char(10);
land1        : abap.char(2);
country_desc : abap.char(40);
po_release   : abap.char(1); 
migo_status  : abap.char(40); 
email        : abap.char(60); 
lastdatetime : timestampl; 
}

Key points:--

  • EBELN is the primary key. 
  • lastdatetime acts as the ETag for draft handling and concurrency control. 
  • po_release indicates whether the PO has been released or not. 

Item Table: ZBRT_T_PO_ITEM--

This stores item-level details linked to the header via a foreign key. 

@EndUserText.label : 'Purchase order item table' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zbrt_t_po_item { 

key mandt       : abap.clnt not null; 
@AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : false 
key ebeln       : ebeln not null 
with foreign key [1..*,1] zbrt_t_po_hrd 
where mandt = zbrt_t_po_item.mandt 
and ebeln = zbrt_t_po_item.ebeln; 
key ebelp       : abap.numc(5) not null; 
matnr           : abap.char(40); 
werks           : abap.char(4); 
lgort           : abap.char(4); 
@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'zbrt_t_po_item.meins' 
menge           : abap.quan(12,2); 
meins           : abap.unit(3); 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zbrt_t_po_item.waers'
netpr           : abap.curr(15,2);
waers           : abap.cuky;
lastdatechanged : timestampl;
}

Key points:-

  • The association [1..*,1] means: Many items can belong to one header. 
  • Semantic annotations help map units and currencies for UI display. 
  • lastdatechanged helps track data changes per item. 

Abstract Entity: ZBRT_POPUP-

Abstract entities are not physical tables; they are used for data transfer or parameter passing. 

In this case, it’s used as a parameter popup entity during your action execution (e.g., PO release). 

@EndUserText.label: 'PO RELEASE' 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 
define abstract entity zbrt_popup 
{ 
@UI.defaultValue: 'X' @EndUserText.label: 'po_release' 
po_release : abap.char( 1 );
}

Purpose:-

  • Used as a popup parameter for a RAP parameterized action. 
  • When the user triggers the Release PO action in the Fiori UI, this abstract entity is displayed to capture additional inputs (like “Release Flag”). 
  • It is decoupled from the database, acting only as a temporary input structure. 

Interface View for Header: ZC_BRT_PO_HEAD-- 

This is the Root Entity of your RAP Business Object. 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection view for header' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S,
dataClass: #MIXED 
} 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 
define root view entity zc_brt_po_head 
as select from zbrt_t_po_hrd 
composition [1..*] of zc_brt_po_item as _item 
{ 
key ebeln,
bukrs,
supplier, 
bsart,
lifnr,
land1,
country_desc,
po_release,
migo_status,
email, 
@Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true 
lastdatetime,
cast('3' as abap.char(1)) as porelease,
cast('1' as abap.char(1)) as noporelease,  
_item
}

Key concepts:-

  • root view entity: Marks it as the root node of the RAP Business Object. 
  • composition [1..*] of _item: Establishes a header–item relationship (1 header → many items). 
  • @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: Enables ETag-based draft and lock management. 
  • cast('3' as abap.char(1)) as porelease:  Defines a calculated field named porelease. This is a constant value ('3') cast to a character of length 1. It is typically used to represent a fixed feature control value (e.g., enabling a 'Release PO' button on the UI). 
  • cast('1' as abap.char(1)) as noporelease: Defines another calculated field named noporelease with a constant value of '1'. Similar to above, this could be used for feature control (e.g., disabling a 'No Release' button). 

Interface View for Item: ZC_BRT_PO_ITEM-- 

This is the child entity linked to the header. 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE] 
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection view for item' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{ 
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S,
dataClass: #MIXED 
} 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 
define view entity zc_brt_po_item
as select from zbrt_t_po_item
association to parent zc_brt_po_head as _head on $projection.ebeln = _head.ebeln
{
key ebeln,
key ebelp,
matnr,
werks,
lgort,
@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Meins'
menge,
meins,
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Waers'
netpr,
waers, 
@Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
lastdatechanged,
_head
}

Explanation:-

  • This view represents the item-level node in your RAP BO. 
  • _head is a public association to navigate back to the header. 
  • The same ETag annotation (@Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt) ensures concurrency management for items too. 

Then design the behavior definition-

managed implementation in class zbp_c_brt_po_head unique; 
strict ( 2 ); 
with draft; 

define behavior for zc_brt_po_head //alias <alias_name> 
persistent table zbrt_t_po_hrd 
lock master 
total etag Lastdatetime 
draft table zbrt_t_h_dft 
authorization master ( instance ) 
etag master lastdatetime 
{
create ( precheck ) { default function GetDefaultsForCreate; } 
update ( precheck, features : instance ); 
delete ( precheck, features : instance ); 
field ( readonly ) bsart, Country_Desc; 
field ( mandatory ) bukrs; 
field ( readonly : create ) po_release;
 
association _item { create; with draft; }
 
mapping for zbrt_t_po_hrd 
{ 
bsart        = bsart; 
bukrs        = bukrs; 
country_desc = country_desc; 
ebeln        = ebeln; 
land1        = land1; 
lastdatetime = lastdatetime; 
lifnr        = lifnr; 
migo_status  = migo_status; 
porelease    = po_release; 
email        = email; 
} 
validation check_email on save { field Email; }
determination migo_status_update on modify { field porelease; } 
action ( features : instance ) POREL parameter zbrt_popup result [1] $self; 
action ( features : instance ) PONOREL result [1] $self;
static action Static_ALL result [1] $self;

draft action Activate optimized;
draft action Edit;
draft action Resume;
draft action Discard;
draft determine action Prepare; 
} 
define behavior for zc_brt_po_item //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zbrt_t_po_item
lock dependent by _head
draft table zbrt_t_i_ft
authorization dependent by _head
etag master Lastdatechanged
{
update;
delete;
field ( readonly ) ebeln, ebelp;
association _head; 
}
determination migo_status_update on modify { field porelease; }

Automatically updates MIGO status whenever the PO Release field changes.

action ( features : instance ) POREL parameter zbrt_popup result [1] $self; 
action ( features : instance ) PONOREL result [1] $self; 
static action Static_ALL result [1] $self;
  • POREL → Triggered to release a purchase order (with parameter popup). 
  • PONOREL → Reverses release. 
  • Static_ALL → Static action (not tied to any instance). 

Metadata extension--

@Metadata.layer: #CORE 
@UI:{ 
    headerInfo:{ 
    title:{ value: 'Ebeln'}, 
    description:{value: 'Migo_Status' }, 
    typeName: 'Purchase Order Data', 
    typeNamePlural: 'Purchase Order Data' } 
} 
annotate view zc_brt_po_head with 
{ 
@UI.facet: 
[ 
{ position: 10, label: 'PO Header', type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE }, 
{ position: 20 , label: 'PO Item' , type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE , targetElement: '_item' } 
] 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 10, label: 'PO Number' 
} , 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION_GROUP, label: 'PO Release Options', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' }, 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'PO Release', dataAction: 'POREL', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' }, 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'PO NotRelease', dataAction: 'PONOREL', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' } 
] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 10, label: 'PO Number' 
} , 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION_GROUP, label: 'PO Release Options', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' }, 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'PO Release', dataAction: 'POREL', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' }, 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'PO NotRelease', dataAction: 'PONOREL', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' } , 

//This button we will use as static button 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'Release All POs', dataAction: 'Static_ALL',actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' } ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 10 
} ] 
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Number' 
ebeln; 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 20 , 
label: 'Company Code' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 20 , 
label: 'Company Code' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 20 
} ] 
@EndUserText.label: 'Company Code' 
bukrs; 
@EndUserText.label: 'Document Type' 
@UI.identification: [ {
position: 40 , 
label: 'Document Type' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 40 , 
label: 'Document Type' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 40 
} ]
bsart;
@EndUserText.label: 'Vendor' 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 50 , 
label: 'Vendor' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 50 , 
label: 'Vendor' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 50 
} ] 
lifnr; 
@EndUserText.label: 'Country' 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 60 , 
label: 'Country' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 60 , 
label: 'Country' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 60 
} ] 
land1; 
@EndUserText.label: 'Country Name' 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 70 , 
label: 'Country Name' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 70 , 
label: 'Country Name' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 70 
} ] 
country_desc; 
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Release' 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 80 , 
label: 'PO Release' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 80 , 
label: 'PO Release' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 80 
} ] 
po_release; 
@EndUserText.label: 'MIGO Status' 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 90 , 
label: 'MIGO Status' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 90 , 
label: 'MIGO Status' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 90 
} ] 
migo_status;
@EndUserText.label: 'Email ID' 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 100 , 
label: 'Email ID' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 100 , 
label: 'Email ID' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 100 
} ]
email; 
@UI.identification: [ { 
position: 110, label: 'Change Date Time' 
} ] 
@UI.lineItem: [ { 
position: 110, label: 'Change Date Time' 
} ] 
@UI.selectionField: [ { 
position: 110 
} ] 
@EndUserText.label: 'Change Date Time'
lastdatetime;
}

This is the Metadata Extension (UI annotations) for RAP Purchase Order application-- it defines how the fields and actions from our behavior definition (zc_brt_po_head) appear in the Fiori Elements app (List Report/Object Page). 

.identification: [ { 

position: 10, label: 'PO Number' 
} , 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION_GROUP, label: 'PO Release Options', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' }, 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'PO Release', dataAction: 'POREL', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' }, 
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'PO NotRelease', dataAction: 'PONOREL', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' } 
]

These create grouped action buttons and drop-down on both the List Report and Object Page:-

  • PO Release Options → Acts as a dropdown group. 
  • Inside it: 
  • PO Release → Triggers the POREL action (for releasing PO). 
  • PO NotRelease → Triggers PONOREL action (for cancelling release). 
  • Release All POs → Added later as a static action.
{ type: #FOR_ACTION, label: 'Release All POs', dataAction: 'Static_ALL', actionGroupId: '_ClickRelease' }

This button executes the Static Action (Static_ALL) — useful for mass operations like “Release All Purchase Orders” without selecting individual ones. 

Then implement the logic in the behavior implementation class for validation, actions and determination-- 

CLASS lhc_zc_brt_po_head DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler. 

PRIVATE SECTION. 

METHODS get_instance_features FOR INSTANCE FEATURES 
IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_features FOR zc_brt_po_head RESULT result. 

METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION 
IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR zc_brt_po_head RESULT result. 

METHODS precheck_create FOR PRECHECK 
IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE zc_brt_po_head. 

METHODS precheck_update FOR PRECHECK 
IMPORTING entities FOR UPDATE zc_brt_po_head. 

METHODS precheck_delete FOR PRECHECK 
IMPORTING keys FOR DELETE zc_brt_po_head. 

METHODS GetDefaultsForCreate FOR READ 
IMPORTING keys FOR FUNCTION zc_brt_po_head~GetDefaultsForCreate RESULT result. 

METHODS ponorel FOR MODIFY 
IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION zc_brt_po_head~ponorel RESULT result. 

METHODS porel FOR MODIFY 
IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION zc_brt_po_head~porel RESULT result.
 
METHODS Static_ALL FOR MODIFY 
IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION zc_brt_po_head~Static_ALL RESULT result. 

METHODS migo_status_update FOR DETERMINE ON MODIFY 
IMPORTING keys FOR zc_brt_po_head~migo_status_update. 

METHODS check_email FOR VALIDATE ON SAVE 
IMPORTING keys FOR zc_brt_po_head~check_email. 

ENDCLASS. 

CLASS lhc_zc_brt_po_head IMPLEMENTATION. 
 
METHOD get_instance_features. 
ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD get_instance_authorizations. 
ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD precheck_create. 

 LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(ls_enity). 

  IF ls_enity-bukrs EQ '6000'. 
    APPEND VALUE #( ebeln = ls_enity-ebeln ) TO failed-zc_brt_po_head. 
    APPEND VALUE #( 
                  ebeln = ls_enity-ebeln 
                  %msg = new_message_with_text(
                  severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                  text = 'Please enter correct Company Code' && ls_enity- bukrs ) )
   TO reported-zc_brt_po_head. 

ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 

ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD precheck_update. 

LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(ls_enity). 

  IF ls_enity-bukrs EQ '6000'. 
   APPEND VALUE #( ebeln = ls_enity-ebeln ) TO failed-zc_brt_po_head. 
   APPEND VALUE #( 
                ebeln = ls_enity-ebeln 
                %msg = new_message_with_text( 
                severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error 
                text = 'Please enter correct Company Code' && ls_enity-Bukrs )
) TO reported-zc_brt_po_head. 

ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 

ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD precheck_delete. 

   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_key IN keys 
                          ( ebeln = ls_key-ebeln ) ) 
RESULT DATA(lt_prechk_delete). 

LOOP AT lt_prechk_delete INTO DATA(ls_enity). 

  IF ls_enity-porelease EQ abap_true. 
    APPEND VALUE #( ebeln = ls_enity-ebeln ) TO failed-zc_brt_po_head. 
    APPEND VALUE #( 
               ebeln = ls_enity-ebeln 
               %msg = new_message_with_text( 
               severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error 
               text = 'You can not delete this line Item as PO release is X'
) 
) TO reported-zc_brt_po_head. 

ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 

ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD GetDefaultsForCreate. 
  APPEND VALUE #( %cid = keys[ 1 ]-%cid %param-bsart = 'ZNB' ) TO result. 
ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD ponorel. 
 
   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_key IN keys 
                          ( ebeln = ls_key-ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_po_action). 

LOOP AT lt_po_action ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_po_action>). 

   MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   UPDATE FIELDS ( porelease ) 
   WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = <fs_po_action>-ebeln 
                   porelease = abap_false ) )  
   REPORTED reported 
   FAILED failed. 

   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = <fs_po_action>-ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_result). 
   result = VALUE #( FOR ls_result IN lt_result 
                   ( ebeln           = ls_result-ebeln 
                    %param-ebeln     = ls_result-ebeln 
                    %param-porelease = ls_result-porelease ) ). 
ENDLOOP. 

ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD porel. 

LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_po_action>). 

   DATA(lv_popup_value) = <fs_po_action>-%param-po_release. 
   MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   UPDATE FIELDS ( po_release ) 
   WITH VALUE #( ( Ebeln = <fs_po_action>-Ebeln 
                   po_release = lv_popup_value ) ) 
   REPORTED reported FAILED failed.

   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( Ebeln = <fs_po_action>-Ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_result). 
   
   result = VALUE #( FOR ls_result IN lt_result 
                   ( ebeln = ls_result-Ebeln 
                     %param-ebeln = ls_result-ebeln 
                     %param-po_release = ls_result-po_release ) ). 

ENDLOOP. 

ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD Static_ALL. 

   SELECT FROM zbrt_t_po_hrd 
          FIELDS ebeln , po_release 
          WHERE po_release = _false 
   INTO TABLE(lt_static_act). 

LOOP AT lt_static_act INTO DATA(ls_static_action). 

   MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   UPDATE FIELDS ( porelease ) 
   WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = ls_static_action-ebeln 
                   porelease = abap_true ) ) .
 
   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = ls_static_action-ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_result). 
   result = VALUE #( FOR ls_result IN lt_result 
                   ( %param-ebeln = ls_result-ebeln 
                     %param-porelease = ls_result-porelease ) ).
ENDLOOP. 

   INSERT VALUE #( 
                 %msg = new_message_with_text( 
                  severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success 
                  text = |{ lines( lt_static_act ) } Records has been  upadted | ) ) 
   INTO TABLE reported-zc_brt_po_head. 

ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD migo_status_update. 

   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_key IN keys 
                          ( ebeln = ls_key-ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_migo_status). 

LOOP AT lt_migo_status ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_migo>). 

  IF <fs_migo>-porelease EQ abap_true. 
     MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
     UPDATE FIELDS ( migo_status )
     WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = <fs_migo>-ebeln 
                     migo_status = 'GR has done' ) ). 

  ELSEIF <fs_migo>-porelease EQ abap_false. 
     MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
     UPDATE FIELDS ( migo_status ) 
     WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = <fs_migo>-ebeln 
                     migo_status = 'Open' ) ). 
ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 
ENDMETHOD. 

METHOD check_email. 

DATA: lr_reg TYPE REF TO cl_abap_regex. 
DATA: lr_matcher TYPE REF TO cl_abap_matcher, 
      lv_email TYPE string. 

   CREATE OBJECT lr_reg
      EXPORTING 
        pattern = '\w+(\.\w+)*@(\w+\.)+(\w{2,4})' 
        ignore_case = abap_true.

   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_key IN keys 
                          ( Ebeln = ls_key-Ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_validation). 

LOOP AT lt_validation INTO DATA(ls_validation). 
   DATA(lv_po_count) = strlen( ls_validation-Email ). 
   IF lv_po_count LT 10. 
     APPEND VALUE #( Ebeln = ls_validation-Ebeln ) TO failed-zc_brt_po_head. 
     APPEND VALUE #( Ebeln = ls_validation-Ebeln 
                     %msg = new_message_with_text( 
                     severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error 
                     text = 'Pls enter correct length for Email' && ls_validation-Email ) ) 
     TO reported-zc_brt_po_head. 

ENDIF. 

   lr_matcher = lr_reg->create_matcher( text = ls_validation-Email ).
   IF lr_matcher->match( ) IS INITIAL.
     APPEND VALUE #( Ebeln = ls_validation-Ebeln ) TO failed-zc_brt_po_head. 
     APPEND VALUE #( Ebeln = ls_validation-Ebeln 
                     %msg = new_message_with_text( 
                     severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error 
                     text = 'Pls enter correct format for Email Id' && ls_validation-Email ) ) 
     TO reported-zc_brt_po_head.
 
ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 
ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.
METHOD ponorel. 
 
  READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
  ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_key IN keys 
  ( ebeln = ls_key-ebeln ) ) RESULT DATA(lt_po_action). 

LOOP AT lt_po_action ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_po_action>). 

   MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   UPDATE FIELDS ( porelease ) 
   WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = <fs_po_action>-ebeln 
   porelease = abap_false 
   ) 
)  REPORTED reported 
   FAILED failed. 
   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = <fs_po_action>-ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_result). 
   result = VALUE #( FOR ls_result IN lt_result 
    ( 
    ebeln            = ls_result-ebeln 
    %param-ebeln     = ls_result-ebeln 
    %param-porelease = ls_result-porelease 
  )
).
ENDLOOP. 

ENDMETHOD.

Purpose of the Method--

ponorel is triggered when a user clicks the “PO Not Release” button in Fiori. 
Its job is to: 

  1. Read the selected PO(s) from the frontend. 
  2. Update their porelease field to false.
  3. Return the updated PO information back to the frontend so the UI reflects the change. 

Step 

Purpose 

Read entity from local mode

Fetch selected POs from draft/local buffer 

Loop through POs 

Process each PO individually 

Modify entity 

Set porelease = false (Not Released) 

Read back updated record 

Get latest values for UI 

Assign to result 

Send updated record(s) back to Fiori 

METHOD porel. 

LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_po_action>). 

   DATA(lv_popup_value) = <fs_po_action>-%param-po_release. 
   MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head UPDATE FIELDS ( po_release ) 
   WITH VALUE #( 
       ( Ebeln = <fs_po_action>-Ebeln po_release = lv_popup_value ) 
  ) 
         REPORTED reported FAILED failed.

   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( Ebeln = <fs_po_action>-Ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_result). 

   result = VALUE #( FOR ls_result IN lt_result 
                   ( ebeln = ls_result-Ebeln 
                    %param-ebeln = ls_result-ebeln 
                    %param-po_release = ls_result-po_release ) ). 
ENDLOOP. 

ENDMETHOD.

Purpose of the Method--

porel is triggered when a user clicks the “PO Release” button in Fiori. 
Its job is to: 

  1. Read the selected POs from the frontend. 
  2. Update their po_release field based on the value passed from the frontend (%param-po_release).
  3. Return the updated PO(s) back to the frontend so the UI reflects the change. 

Feature 

ponorel 

porel 

po_release update 

Always sets to false 

Sets dynamically from frontend %param-po_release 

Use case 

“PO Not Release” button 

“PO Release” button or popup 

Logic 

Hard-coded 

Dynamic based on action parameter 

METHOD Static_ALL. 

   SELECT FROM zbrt_t_po_hrd 
          FIELDS ebeln , po_release 
          WHERE po_release = _false 
   INTO TABLE (lt_static_act). 

LOOP AT lt_static_act INTO DATA(ls_static_action). 

   MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   UPDATE FIELDS ( porelease ) 
   WITH VALUE #( 
                ( ebeln = ls_static_action-ebeln 
                  porelease = abap_true )
 ) . 
   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = ls_static_action-ebeln ) ) 
   RESULT DATA(lt_result). 

   result = VALUE #( FOR ls_result IN lt_result 
                   ( 
                    %param-ebeln = ls_result-ebeln 
                    %param-porelease = ls_result-porelease 
                   )
).
ENDLOOP. 

   INSERT VALUE #( 
               %msg = new_message_with_text( 
               severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-success 
               text = |{ lines( lt_static_act ) } Records has been upadted | 
                ) 
) INTO TABLE reported-zc_brt_po_head. 

ENDMETHOD.

Purpose of Static_ALL-- 

  • This method is typically triggered by a “Release All POs” button in Fiori. 
  • It automatically sets all POs with porelease = false to true. 
  • Provides a success message for the user showing how many POs were updated.

Step 

Action 

1 

Select all unreleased POs from DB 

2 

Loop through them 

3 

Set porelease = true in local/draft mode 

4 

Read the updated record 

5 

Return updated data to frontend 

6 

Insert a success message in reported-zc_brt_po_head 

METHOD migo_status_update. 

   READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
   ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #
                    ( FOR ls_key IN keys 
                    ( ebeln = ls_key-ebeln ) ) 
                      RESULT DATA(lt_migo_status). 

LOOP AT lt_migo_status ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_migo>). 

   IF <fs_migo>-porelease EQ abap_true. 
      MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
      UPDATE FIELDS ( migo_status )
      WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = <fs_migo>-ebeln 
      migo_status = 'GR has done') ).

   ELSEIF <fs_migo>-porelease EQ abap_false. 
      MODIFY ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE zc_brt_po_head 
      UPDATE FIELDS ( migo_status ) 
      WITH VALUE #( ( ebeln = <fs_migo>-ebeln 
      migo_status = 'Open' ) ). 

ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 
ENDMETHOD.

Purpose--

  • Whenever the field porelease is modified (via POREL, PONOREL, or Static_ALL), this determination triggers. 
  • Updates migo_status dynamically: 
  • 'GR has done' → if the PO is released (porelease = abap_true). 
  • 'Open' → if the PO is not released (porelease = abap_false). 

Concept 

Explanation 

Determination 

Runs automatically when a field changes (porelease) in RAP. 

Local Mode 

Updates the draft version, ensuring changes are visible in the UI before committing. 

Dynamic Update 

migo_status is computed automatically based on porelease. 

No result table 

The UI will automatically get updated via RAP binding; no explicit result table needed. 

Create the service definition and service binding--

Let's preview our application in Fiori--

Above can see the PO Release status and MIGO Status is done. 

Let's create a record and see the pop-up.

Above can the action button with drop down. 

Let’s check the actions button, how PO release and MIGO status are changing. 

Select one record-->click on action drop-down-->click on PO Not Release. 

Now can see automatically MIGO status changed based on PO Release. 

Conclusion--

In this blog, we explored how to implement parameterized actions in RAP using abstract entities and a dropdown-based parameter selection, enabling flexible and user-driven behavior in Fiori applications. 

We demonstrated how multiple actions -- such as POREL, PONOREL, and STATIC_ALL — can be designed to handle different business scenarios like Purchase Order release, mass update, and status refresh dynamically. Furthermore, with the MIGO_STATUS_UPDATE determination, we ensured real-time consistency between release and goods receipt statuses, illustrating the power of behavior-driven logic in the RAP framework.

Thanks-------

entry.title: Planning your transition paths to SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Planning a move to SAP Business Data Cloud isn’t just a technical exercise.  It’s a business conversation.  And honestly, the right architecture choices early on make everything smoother later.

In our upcoming partner webinar, “Planning your transition paths to SAP Business Data Cloud,” we’ll walk through real, practical architecture options that help you modernize with confidence—without forcing a one-size-fits-all approach.

We’ll explore when a greenfield approach makes sense, when phased migration is the smarter play, and how SAP HANA Cloud and SAP BDC work together through bi-directional data exchange to unlock hybrid innovation. Plus, we’ll connect the dots across SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Databricks, and data products that actually drive decisions, not just dashboards.

The thing is: partners who succeed here don’t just implement technology.  They guide customers through the why, the how, and the what’s next.

Join us to learn best practices, value realization strategies, and how SAP assessments can support your transition planning.

👉 Register now  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jul/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010869.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #BusinessDataCloud #SAPArchitecture #DataStrategy

entry.title: Re: Email notifications should be sent whenever a background job is cancelled or fails.
entry.content:
@vinodkumarkvk  you are right. The Basis team has confirmed that the standard setup can send emails for all job failures, but it does not have the capability to send emails to separate mail IDs for a specific job failure.

To achieve this, we may need to develop a custom report and schedule it in the background. This report would monitor the specified job(s) and send email notifications to the listed users in case of job failure.

entry.title: Re: Extract a measure depending on a max measure
entry.content:
No problem 🙂

I can't find the "Period" column in the screenshot you shared, but I assume it's on a day-level.

You can add "Period" (or CALDAY or whatever your period column is called) to the window function:

case 
    when qta colli = MAX(qta colli) OVER (PARTITION by Centro, Codice Materiale, Period) then 
        tempo lordo lavorato
    else 
        0
    end

Which should do the trick.

The window function, such as MAX(), will use the "Partition" clause to calculate the value. 
But(!) it's important to note that all columns not in the Partition will be ignored. 

So, if you add your period - it should all be good 🙂

entry.title: What's New in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 Analytics - January 2026
entry.content:
As part of our monthly releases during Q4 2025, we have introduced a new set of analytics innovations across SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 and SAP Enterprise Service Management. These enhancements reflect our continued investment in scalable, global analytics and address some of the most frequently requested capabilities from our customers.

The Q4 focus was clear: extend analytics beyond a single product, enable global usability, and empower organizations with more flexible, enterprise-grade insights. This release delivers on that vision with three key innovations:

  • Metadata Translation Support for Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Stories
  • Currency Conversion 1.0
  • Live Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud, Enterprise Edition (Restricted GA)

Below is a closer look at how each of these enhancements delivers tangible business value.

Metadata Translation Support for Embedded SAC Stories

SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 and SAP Enterprise Service Management are deployed globally, supporting users across regions, languages, and cultures. To ensure consistent and localized user experiences, both solutions offer Language Adaptation, enabling administrators to translate field labels, code lists, UI texts, and custom messages.

Until now, Embedded SAC supported data translations and translations created directly within SAC’s Query Designer and Story Designer. With the November 2025 release, metadata translations maintained centrally in the Language Adaptation tool are now automatically applied to Embedded SAC Stories.

Business impact:

  • Single point of translation maintenance across operational UIs and analytics
  • Consistent multilingual experiences for business users
  • Reduced administrative overhead by eliminating duplicate translation efforts

All translated field-related metadata is seamlessly reflected in the Embedded SAC user interface, ensuring analytics remain as intuitive and localized as the underlying applications.

You can see how this works in this video.

Currency Conversion 1.0

For organizations operating across multiple regions, currency conversion is a foundational analytics requirement—particularly in sales and service reporting. With the December 2025 release, we introduce Currency Conversion 1.0 for Embedded SAC.

Administrators can now define a target currency for amount-based attributes:

  • During data modeling in the Query Designer
  • Directly in the Story Designer for specific charts or tables

Different target currencies can be applied within the same story, offering greater flexibility for global and regional reporting.

Key capabilities and considerations:

  • Centralized rate maintenance: Conversion rates maintained in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 or SAP Enterprise Service Management are automatically used in SAC stories.
  • Automatic rate selection: Conversion rates are applied at runtime; users do not manage conversion datasets manually.
  • Modeling requirement: Both amount and currency attributes must be included in the data source for conversion to work correctly.

This capability enables consistent, accurate financial reporting while preserving a single source of truth for currency data. Stay tuned for the ability to select a currency conversion date as the next enhancement to this feature!

You can see how this works in this video.

Live Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud, Enterprise Edition (Restricted GA)

Modern enterprises rely on multiple SAP solutions—such as SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 for CRM and SAP SuccessFactors for HCM—and require a unified analytics layer across systems. SAP Analytics Cloud, Enterprise Edition fulfills this role.

With the December 2025 release, SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 and SAP Enterprise Service Management can now integrate with Enterprise SAC via live connections.

Key benefits of live integration:

  • Real-time insights: Data is accessed directly from the source systems during story execution.
  • Enhanced data security: Data remains within the source system and is discarded once the story is closed.
  • Complete data access: All available data sources in Sales & Service Cloud V2 and Enterprise Service Management are accessible in Enterprise SAC.
  • Advanced analytics and modeling: Leverage Enterprise SAC capabilities to further model and visualize operational data.

This feature is currently available under restricted general availability. Customers interested in early access can request enablement by creating a case under component CEC-CRM-ANA. Broader availability is planned in the near future.

You can see how this works in this video.

Help documentation from SAP Analytics Cloud is available for this as well & can be accessed here & here.

Enabling Truly Global, Enterprise-Grade Analytics

Together, these enhancements significantly strengthen the analytics foundation of SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 and SAP Enterprise Service Management. By improving multilingual support, enabling multi-currency reporting, and unlocking enterprise-wide analytics through live integration, we are helping customers scale insights globally—without compromising consistency, security, or performance.

These innovations represent another step toward delivering analytics that are not only powerful, but truly global.

entry.title: Re: WM Monitor queues WMQF* state SYSFAIL with no informa...
entry.content:
Good day @isolde_grammfe1fd5 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is a typical EWM QM processing issue.

I suggest you first identify what the queue is trying to execute.

Open your Warehouse Monitor, tools --> Message Queue.

Find the stuck queue and execute

Open the message details for that queue entry. Note the details.

The error message “No display program registered for this queue” Message no. /SCWM/MONITOR055” means that the monitor cannot jump to a dedicated display program for that queue prefix. It does not mean there is technically an error. You should still find out the trouble for WMQF.

As described in KBA 3301813, this is mostly caused by a few factors:

  • Your storage type setting blocks Q unrestricted posting. So, check your EWM customizing. Particularly Maintain Storage Types -->  Putaway Control. If the destination storage type is used for Q to unrestricted postings, then ensure you did not set “No Mixed Stock Types”.
  • Check your Putaway strategy and destination storage type determination.
  • Your stock may be blocked by an open task in your warehouse or bin is blocked.
  • Check Maintain Follow Up Actions app, verify the HU WT related flags are consistent with your process.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Understanding the New Tags in the SAF-T Poland VAT Report (JPK_V7M(3) and JPK_V7K(3))
entry.content:
Poland's tax reporting landscape is evolving, and SAP has introduced new tags in the Standard Audit File for Tax (SAF-T) structures JPK_V7M(3) and JPK_V7K(3) to help businesses stay compliant with the country's electronic invoicingrequirements. 

Understanding the Four New Tags 

In this blog article, we' ll explain what the new tags mean and how to use them, along with examples to make things clearer. 

The new structures JPK_V7M(3) and JPK_V7K(3) of the Polish SAF-T VAT report now include the following tags designed to simplify the reporting process: 

  • "NrKSeF": This tag is the KSeF Reference Number, which serves as an identifier for invoices in the National e-Invoice System (KSeF).  
  • "OFF": Use this tag for invoices referred to in Article 106nf(1) of the act that don't have a KSeF identification number and haven't been submitted to KSeF.  
  • "BFK": This applies to either electronic or paper invoices not categorized as e-invoices by KSeF, for example in cases where it’s not mandatory to issue invoices using the National e-Invoice System.  
  • "DI": Represents any proof of transaction other than an invoice issued through the KSeF. This applies to all other cases of documents that will not have a KSeF number, have not been sent to KSeF and can’t be categorized as ‘BFK’ or ‘OFF’.

Examples 

To help companies meet the new standards, let's look at examples of where these tags are applicable. 

The ‘NrKSeF’ Tag 

Use the "NrKSeF" tag when there's a KSeF Reference Number for an invoice. This number identifies the invoice within the National e-Invoice System. For example, if you're processing an invoice registered in KSeF, you should tag it with the appropriate "NrKSeF" number. 

Complete this field if, as of the date of submission of JPK_VAT, the invoice has been assigned a KSeF number. 

The ‘OFF’ Tag 

Apply the "OFF" tag to invoices referred to in Article 106nf(1) of the act that don’t have a KSeF number as of the date of submission of the records.  

This designation applies to invoices issued during the period of malfunction of the National e-Invoice System, according to Art.106nf(1). This designation indicates that, due to the inactivity of this system, the invoice does not have a KSeF identifying number as of the date of submission of the JPK_VAT. 

If an invoice exists in e-Document processing but still lacks a KSeF Reference Number, mark it as "OFF". 

The ‘BFK’ Tag 

The "BFK" tag is for invoices not handled by KSeF, such as paper invoices, vendor invoices for foreign transactions or invoices with document types that are excluded from KSeF. This category also includes invoices from cash registers, invoices from consumers in B2C relations issued outside of KSeF, invoices for financial services using KSeF exemption, etc.  

This tag allows you to indicate transactions that for various legal reasons fall outside of the KSeF.  

If the document is not marked with DI, OFF or KSeF th en it is reported by JPK_V7K(3) and JPK_V7M(3) with status BFK.

The ‘DI’ Tag 

Use the "DI" tag for all other documents that serve as transaction proofs but are not an invoice and do not qualify as ‘OFF’ or ‘BFK’.  

For example, daily reports from cash registers, internal documents, customs documents, or other accounting documents.  

Invoices issued in the offline24 mode must also be tagged with the ‘DI’ tag.  

DI should be also assigned to invoices, which should be registered in KSeF, but due to different reasons cannot be registered. This may happen in the initial period applicable with KSeF, when for various reasons, mainly technological and configuration ones, the taxpayer is unable to register the invoice in KSeF, but will have to forward it to the business partner. 

With SAP Document and Reporting Compliance you can select for which type of transaction DI is relevant. See the example below. 

Why This Matters

These tags help you accurately categorize your transactions, ensuring compliance with Polish tax regulations while reducing reporting errors.  

You can also find detailed information about the changes in the JPK_V7M(3) and JPK_V7K(3) structures in the Regulatory Change Manager. 

For detailed guidance on implementing KSeF requirements in your SAP system, check out the related blog articles. 

  • How Official Document Numbering helps to fulfill the Polish KSeF Requirements in S4HANA Public Cloud 
  • How you can manage Polish KSeF Requirement in SAP S/⁠4HANA Cloud Private Edition & SAP S/⁠4HANA 
  • How to store long Supplier Invoice Reference Numbers in SAP S/4HANA ERP Products 

 Staying updated with the latest legal changes is essential for navigating the ever-evolving taxation landscape. By understanding how to use these tags, you can ensure compliance with national regulations, reduce errors, and streamline your tax processes.
